body.title: [sprint] Filesystem ticket-store module — the single queue chokepoint (no Postgres)
body.description:
**Problem:** Ticket state is read/written from Postgres in ~21 places. To cut over to filesystem-first WITHOUT scattering 21 ad-hoc JSON-parsing reimplementations (fix-one-leave-many), we need ONE chokepoint module — the queue analogue of memory_emit.py — that owns the filesystem ticket store.

**Proposed shape:** NEW module (e.g. unseen_university/ticket_store.py or devlab/runtime/ticket_store.py) exposing: read(ticket_id), list(status_filter=None), write(ticket_dict), set_worker(id, worker), set_status(id, status), close(id, result) [→ moves the JSON file from tickets/ to tickets/closed/], next_for_worker(...). Storage: devlab/runtime/memory/tickets/*.json for in-flight, tickets/closed/*.json for terminal. ALL writes use atomic write-to-temp + rename (PATTERN-004 flat-file registry) — this is what replaces Postgres's lock-safety and avoids the silent-data-loss failure mode that banned SQLite. Match the existing memory_emit envelope schema (id/emitter/namespace/category/kind/emitted_at/links/body). Concurrency: Granny set_worker and CC close may race — atomic rename + read-modify-write with retry.
**Affected files:** NEW ticket_store.py; NEW devlab/runtime/memory/tickets/closed/ dir (create on first close). Unit tests NEW tests for the store.
**Design rules:** NO SQLITE EVER. Atomic write+rename (PATTERN-004). Log every status transition + the close-move (state changes). No Postgres import in this module.
**Scope boundary:** IN — the filesystem store API + atomic-write + closed-bin move + tests. OUT — migrating callers (T-cc-queue-fs-first, T-ticket-readers-migrate); deleting PG (T-ticket-pg-drop).
**Completion criteria:** Module reads/writes/lists/closes tickets purely on the filesystem with Postgres stopped; close() moves the file to closed/; concurrent set_worker+close test passes without corruption; constraint-stamping parity with current add path preserved (or explicitly deferred with a note).
**Test plan:** Unit tests: write→read roundtrip; list filters by status; close moves file to closed/ and removes from active list; two threads (set_worker + close) leave a valid single JSON. Run with PG unreachable to prove no DB dependency.
